topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The turpentine fumes seemed to thicken, mirroring the sudden, sharp clench in my jaw. I stared at the canvas, the vibrant chaos I had painstakingly coaxed from the pigments now marred by an intrusion. A precise, black line, a delicate, yet utterly damning tattoo, traced the outline of my central figure’s eye. My breath hitched, a ragged, shallow sound in the quiet studio. I snatched a brush, the bristles stiff against my palm, and hurled it against the wooden palette. It struck the floor with a dull thud, the sound echoing the violent tremor running through my hands. Someone had dared to claim ownership over my creation. The air felt suddenly too thin, suffocating. I rose, my movements jerky, and paced the cramped space, my boots scraping against the concrete floor. Every shadow seemed to hold the shape of the offender.

[story 2]

It was the sight that stopped me dead. I leaned in close, my heart hammering a frantic rhythm against my ribs. Beneath the swirling blues and ochres of my latest piece, a stark, unfamiliar script was inked onto the subject’s forearm—a symbol I didn't recognize, undeniably someone else’s work. A cold, hard knot tightened in my stomach, spreading outward like ice water. My knuckles turned white where I gripped the edge of the easel. "Who authorized this?" I spat the words out, the tone dangerously low, a vibration that seemed to shake the very glass in the window. I took a step back, breathing in short, sharp gasps. This was an invasion, a brazen theft of my vision, and the sheer audacity of it made my vision swim. I turned abruptly, the sound of my chair scraping backward a grating protest against the silence.

[story 3]

My eyes narrowed to slits as I examined the detail. It wasn't just a mark; it was a deliberate signature, placed with an arrogant familiarity onto the very heart of what I had poured into that canvas. A hot flush crept up my neck, staining my cheeks crimson. I felt a surge of raw, untamed energy coiling in my chest, making my pulse throb against my collarbone like a trapped bird. I walked to the window, pressing my forehead against the cool glass, trying to steady the internal storm. I could feel the need to lash out, to destroy the evidence, but I held myself rigid, forcing the energy down into a tight, controlled burn. When I finally turned back, my gaze was fixed, unwavering, radiating a palpable sense of profound offense. I didn't speak; the silence between us was loud enough to crack stone.
